content: The world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.
author: Albert Einstein
description: Our external reality is a reflection of our internal thought patterns; to change the world, we must first change our mindset.

content: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Character is defined by the decisions we make rather than our innate talents or potential.

content: There are only two ways to live your life. One is as though nothing is a miracle. The other is as though everything is a miracle.
author: Albert Einstein
description: Life can be viewed through the lens of cynicism or through the lens of awe and wonder.

content: The person, be it gentleman or lady, who has not pleasure in a good novel, must be intolerably stupid.
author: Jane Austen
description: A lighthearted jab suggesting that reading literature is an essential part of an intelligent life.

content: Imperfection is beauty, madness is genius and it's better to be absolutely ridiculous than absolutely boring.
author: Marilyn Monroe
description: Embracing one's authentic, quirky self is more valuable than conforming to standard norms of behavior.

content: Try not to become a man of success. Rather become a man of value.
author: Albert Einstein
description: Focusing on contributing value to others is more meaningful than simply chasing personal success.

content: It is better to be hated for what you are than to be loved for what you are not.
author: André Gide
description: Authenticity is more important than being liked based on a false persona.

content: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
author: Thomas A. Edison
description: Persistence and re-framing mistakes as learning experiences are keys to innovation.

content: A woman is like a tea bag; you never know how strong it is until it's in hot water.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt
description: True strength and resilience are revealed when facing difficult or challenging situations.

content: A day without sunshine is like, you know, night.
author: Steve Martin
description: A humorous observation stating an obvious fact in a mocking way.
